No Poverty.reason: The course's focus on real estate management, including property concepts, has a very tangential link to Target 1.4 regarding access to and control over land and property, even if not directly focused on poverty alleviation.

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to ending hunger, improving nutrition, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to health, well-being, or medical services.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides tertiary education and professional skills in real estate management, aligning strongly with Targets 4.3 and 4.4.
evidence: 培養不動產經營管理所需之專業能力
建立未來從事實務工作之良好基礎

Gender Equality.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to clean water, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to affordable, clean, or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly covers real estate's role in the economy and business cycles, directly supporting economic growth, productivity, and job creation in the real estate sector (Targets 8.1, 8.2, 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course directly addresses the management of real estate development, office buildings, and industrial real estate, which are core components of infrastructure and industrialization (Targets 9.1, 9.2).
evidence: 工業不動產等之經營管理
辦公大樓、購物中心...之經營管理
management of... office buildings, shopping centers, ... industrial real estate

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to reducing inequalities in income, social inclusion, or opportunities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course is fundamentally about managing various types of real estate (residential, commercial, industrial) which are core components of urban and human settlements, directly supporting sustainable cities and communities (Targets 11.1, 11.3).
evidence: 住宅、辦公大樓、購物中心...之經營管理
management of... residential, office buildings, shopping centers

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit evidence in the syllabus directly addresses responsible consumption, sustainable production patterns, or resource efficiency in real estate.

Climate Action.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or resilience.

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to marine life, oceans, or coastal ecosystems.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit evidence in the syllabus directly addresses terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or sustainable land use beyond the scope of property management.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to peace, justice, rule of law, or strong, accountable institutions.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: While teamwork and an international perspective are mentioned, the course does not explicitly address global partnerships, finance, technology, or trade for sustainable development as defined by SDG 17.
